reasoning: Circle Internet Group filed its public S-1 registration statement with the SEC on April 1, 2025. Multiple reputable news sources (including Reuters, PYMNTS, and CNBC) reported in April 2025 that the company is targeting an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: CRCL) by June 2025. The typical timeline from a public filing to an IPO debut is 4-12 weeks, making a late June listing highly plausible. As of the May 14 cutoff, there have been no reports of delays, regulatory hurdles, or market-driven postponements that would derail the June target. Given the momentum, the backing of major underwriters (JPMorgan and Citigoup), and the clear public filing, it is very likely the stock will trade before July 1, 2025.
